I want to go through something really important here and that is the art and science of building referrals. Let me just tell you something. I think this is one spot that whatever reason sales have been just fall down on naturally. I don't know if they're scared to ask for referrals or they think their prospects are new clients and they piss with them and they piss with them and they ask for referrals. Let me just tell you the bottom line. I was in the mortgage business. My business very quickly after a few months was almost strictly referral. Why? Because I asked for them. I asked them relentlessly. Now let me go through this step by step here. Very quickly it's just a quick kid video. We'll go into this more deeply on an intensive but when is the business ... think about this. When do you think the best time is to ask someone for referral? So let's say it's like May 1st and you're out there and you're closing on a product. They're done. You sell them. It's a $2,000 product and you shake hands and say, great. You're gonna love this, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah. The question is do you want to ask them right at the table? At the closing table. Either it's in person or over the phone. Or do you want to wait a few days and then you call them back to check on them and say, hey, do you want to make sure everything's going well? Do you have any questions? Do you want to make sure you have a good experience? Oh, by the way, you know anybody else who could use the same? Blah, blah, blah. You want to do it then. Do you need to even wait longer than that? And just for the sake of politeness in general, like just being a good person that you want to wait to give the person a chance to experience the benefits of the product for themselves before they have to recommend to a friend. Which do you think is right? Now hopefully you know a little bit about this program by now and if you said the last one, you know I'm gonna jump through the lens and try to choke you, right? Because it's terrible. You'll destroy yourself. You don't have to wait for any arbitrary amount of time before you start asking for girls. Frankly, bottom line, and I've tested this out over many different industries. I've done this the years. It's the best time to ask for a referral is right after you close the person. That very instinct. Why is that? Well, there's a couple of reasons. Number one is that when someone says yes, right? If once they say yes in order to remain consistent with them saying yes, they won't think they made a smart decision. They can't say, oh yeah, I'll do it. I'll give you my money, but I'm not comfortable enough to recommend a friend. Rarely do you decide a person. Rather that person is gonna actually want to recommend this friend because he wants to form his own consensus. Those consensus is one of those core influencing traits. We want to be part of the crowd. We want to be part of the consensus, right? So it almost is an affirmation that he did the right thing. He made a good decision by going out there and getting someone else to do it too. That's why people will give you referrals right at the closing table. In addition, it's that moment after you close when you just load their actual threshold layer, then very high on the certainty scale, that's when they're most apt to give you a referral. Don't fall into the trap. It's a weakness. It's just being weak and frail. Seriously, wake the f*** up. I want you to make it a f***ing habit. Second you close a deal. Then first words out of your mouth, they're the great. You're gonna love this all by the way. You're right? Who else do you know that I can help in the same way that I helped you? Not who else can you refer to? Who else can, you know, I can help in the same way that I just helped you? Remember, you just helped this person fill in. If you've done this right, you've built massive rapport along the way, right? You're in rapport, you're closed in rapport because you can't close without rapport, right? This is the perfect time. Don't be shy. Don't like chicken abs. It's a mental thing. Just put your f***ing back up with total comps. Oh, by the way, who else do you know that I can benefit in the same way that I helped you? And just shut up. Now, that being said, going forward, there are different levels of referrals. Like the best thing is we'll be, listen, here's what I want you to do. I want you to take out your roll deck. Take out your smart phone and I want you to actually dial number yourself right now in front of me. Here's what I want you to do, John. Take out your phone. I want you to hear your top three people. I'll wait right here. I'm gonna wait here. I want you to dial them and tell them this is the greatest thing in the world and then stand the phone to be empty, told them not to be in front of you. That's your best offer, right? Versus them saying, oh yeah, I'll call my friend Jim who will love this and I'll tell Jim to give you a call. Is that okay? Oh yeah, sure. Here's my cell phone. Give it to Jim. Don't hold your f***ing breath from Jim to call you. That's your work, right? And then in the middle of those is the place. Yeah, yeah, call this guy John Doe when he hears his phone and gives you the number. You might think I was kidding around when I had that first scenario like, all right, give it. I want you to do, John. Take out your f***ing smart phone. Let me tell you something. If you do this right and you're in tight rapport with someone, you can get someone to take out their phone right now and start calling people. I did this all the time. I'm saying, I did someone's house. I closed them on their mortgage. That one is doing the mortgage business. It's a perfect big referral business, right? Closed them on their mortgage. I had not even refinanced their home yet. They had no idea if I was good, bad or indifferent what I did, but they were so confident based on how I sounded. We were in such tight rapport because of my focus of the second 10 and future pace of emotionaling that a person who is typically hostile when I walk, they're doing it with typically a hostile somewhat, right? They were cooking me dinner. I'd be sitting there with a f***ing meal out, wine here, and they're busy on their phone, calling their friends one after the next saying, what a great guy I am. Okay? I'm not a magician. All right? I'm not, I'm not, it's not like I'm Harry Potter's. Like I cast a spell on them. It's very simple. I asked them to do it in a nice way. They got your f***ing smartphone and start dialing Bill. I'm like Bill, honestly, I can't tell you. My business, mostly it's on referral. You know, I call call them probably, right? I still make most of the business on referral. I really appreciate if you, anyone who I can help in the same way, they say, oh yeah, I have my friend, you're great. Do me a favor, please. Please do me a favor. Look him right in the eye. You please give her a call. It's a big difference. Don't flat out. You know it's a big difference if they hear it directly from you versus me calling them. Do you mind? What's so bad about that? Ask and ye shall receive. Don't ask and ye shall not receive. Just ask them nicely, respectfully and with integrity. There's nothing worth it. I'm not saying anything like, you know, you try to be on the hand. I'm saying you have a great product. You help people. Why not help more people? Unless you don't believe in what you're telling. Then you shouldn't be selling it anyway. Come on. Back up. Every sale you close today, I want you to ask for a referral on the spot and get them to dial that phone and pass you the phone or ask and die trying. Because the worst that will happen, you know what I'm saying is not right, but here it is, it's number. That's your default. They're not going to be mad at you if you do it the right way. That's the bottom line. All right. Love your oil. That's a tip for the day. That's one that will put money in your pocket like that. Do it. Talk soon.
